relations.note: Dark adjacency checks are enabled by the background color declarations in section metadata.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; no margins outside the section
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Ensure reliable modular stacking and layout structure across email clients.
rules.rule_text: Each section is a self-contained <table>-based block at 600px (dimension.canvas.width = 600px) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (padding.section.vertical = 32px) and a declared background color. There must be no margins outside the section, allowing direct stacking.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Facilitate automated assembly, legal scoping, and footnote sequencing.
rules.rule_text: Section metadata must explicitly declare the background color, whether the section establishes 'dermatomyositis (DM)', the footnote symbols consumed, and whether it contains a CTA.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at assembly time — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Prevent numbering and symbol conflicts when combining modular email sections.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols must be superscript and assigned dynamically in order of appearance at assembly time. Sections must use placeholder tokens (e.g. {{fn1}}) instead of hard-coded symbols to avoid collisions.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Ensure layout independence and visual safety when stacking against fixed global components.
rules.rule_text: Sections adjacent to locked components (header, footer, ISI, which are inserted by the core team) must not include decorative elements (such as continuing wave graphics) that visually depend on what is above or below them.

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain visual contrast and clear section boundaries in modular designs.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed directly adjacent to other dark-background sections in the final email stack.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Ensure robust and consistent rendering across legacy and modern email clients.
rules.rule_text: All colors must be written as HEX values in code; all sizing must be in px; styles must use inline CSS only; and bgcolor attributes must be duplicated for Outlook compatibility.
